description: Bundled XRD shape-finding algorithms (flat library). These are Phase-2 (cross-bin) algorithms run by 'xrd-app shapes' AFTER per-bin peak detection ('xrd-app peaks'): they link peaks across neighboring spatial bins and keep features with a gaussian-like cross-bin intensity profile (a real Bragg 'shape'). Each exposes link_peaks(...) and characterize_features(...) (and a standalone run_shape_pipeline / main). bin_size null = applies to any binned size. Per-frame combined (peak+shape) algorithms live in the CombinedAlgorithms/ library.
metric: holdout_f1 / holdout_f2 (spatially-validated holdout score; higher is better)

detectors.name: gaussian
detectors.notes: Baseline shape finder: Union-Find cross-bin linking + gaussian-profile filtering (keep clusters with a clear bright center) + characterization (chi FWHM, strain breadth). Generic reference, runs on any binned size.

detectors.name: gaussian_deskew
detectors.notes: Fast 1x1 post-processing skew fix: drop-in for 'gaussian' that corrects serpentine/backlash column misregistration at the LINKING stage (estimates the per-row column shift from unambiguous cross-row matches, links on the corrected column with an adaptive cross-row window), then reuses the gaussian profile filter + characterization verbatim. Same default-grid bin keys, so its catalog is directly comparable to the territorial truth (TERRITORY.md / compare_to_truth.py). Neutral on already-clean (binned) grids.

detectors.name: territory
detectors.notes: Territorial / cell-model source-of-truth linker: same Union-Find + gaussian filter + characterization as 'gaussian', but links across PHYSICAL territory neighbors (true (X,Y) adjacency) instead of the N×N 8-neighborhood. Pair with a territorial grid mapping (core.territory) — 'xrd-app shapes --algorithm territory --variant territory'. Skew-free reference for optimizing the fast post-processing.

detectors.name: voigt
detectors.notes: Voigt shape finder: same linking as gaussian/territory (grid or coordinate, auto-dispatched) but keeps features whose cross-bin radial profile fits a pseudo-Voigt (R2 test + compact/monotonic fallbacks) instead of the gaussian monotonicity check. Ports the Voigt verification from the per-frame CombinedAlgorithms onto the standard peaks->shapes pipeline (full intensities/profiles, populates Device/Orientation). Select with 'xrd-app shapes --algorithm voigt'.
